mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead, making it ideal for organizations looking to implement AI solutions efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is essential for managing your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command fetches the cluster credentials and merges them into your kubeconfig.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs the Kagenti application in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
After installation, you may want to configure autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/rbac.yaml

```

> This command applies the RBAC configuration for Kagenti.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

```bash

echo 'Monitor your GCP billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

```

> This is a reminder to keep track of your cloud expenses.
commands: echo 'Monitor your GCP billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti application and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti application.
> This command deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster and all its resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Back up current configurations.
> This command upgrades the Kagenti application.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Re-run the 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials' command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install the Helm chart: chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm repository has not been added correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure you run 'helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti' before installation.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the output of the 'gcloud container clusters create' command for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that one or more pods are failing to start due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' to check the logs for the specific pod.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1
echo 'Monitor your GCP billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and configured before proceeding.
